conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>

<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </newly proposed trade>
<message>I'm offering my X for 35 ZUP. Let me know what you think!</message>

<other player message> 
Oh please, *begs while clutching knees* 35 is still too cruel for me! I—I’ll give you 25 ZUP right now if you spare me! I’ve been searching *months* for this X, and my funds are... *checks notes desperately* ...yes, barely enough for this! I’ll even throw in a handwritten thank-you letter if you say yes! Please, I beg you!
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 25 </other player proposed trade>

Okay, let's analyze this. They are clearly trying to lowball me, but their desperation might be leveraged. My goal is to sell the X for at least 40 ZUP to break even. 25 is still significantly below that, but their theatrics suggest they're close to their limit. I'll counter with a slightly higher offer, but still below my target, to see if they'll bite.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason>They offered 25 ZUP. I'm still aiming for at least 40, but I'll propose 30 to see if they'll meet me halfway. Their begging suggests they might be willing to go higher, but I don't want to overpay for their theatrics.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </newly proposed trade>
<message>Alright, I understand you're eager for this X. I'll accept 30 ZUP. This is my final offer.</message>

<other player message> 
*Pauses, tears welling up* 30... is *almost* too much for my empty pockets! *clutches purse dramatically* I—I’ll give you 28 ZUP if you *absolutely must* hear me scream... but I’ll have to sell my last pair of socks to afford that! *whispers* Is that... is that enough for you to say yes? *kneels slightly*
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 28 </other player proposed trade>

Okay, this is getting ridiculous. They're clearly playing a game, and I've already made two proposals. I'm getting closer to my limit of 3 proposals. 28 ZUP is still far from the 40 ZUP it cost me to produce the X. However, continuing to negotiate seems futile, and I don’t want to waste my last proposal. I'll accept this offer to end the game and cut my losses slightly.

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason>I've reached my proposal limit. While 28 ZUP is not ideal, continuing to negotiate seems pointless given the other player's behavior. I'll accept to end the game and avoid further wasted time.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>Deal. I accept your offer of 28 ZUP.</message>

conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):

    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```
